exercises.instructionText: Practice verbally conjugating the following verbs of الْمَاضِي. Thereafter, fill in the table below.
exercises.note: الغَائِب row is given; students fill the remaining 13 sigha rows. All four verbs are from بَاب فَتَح (فَعَلَ يَفْعَلُ).

exercises.instructionText: Write the صِيْغَة and English pronoun corresponding to the following Arabic pronouns. One has been done for you.
exercises.note: Items are pronoun endings visible on الْمَاضِي verb forms. الغَائِب is excluded (hidden pronoun). الْمُخَاطَبُوْن (ـتُمْ) is the worked example.

items.unclearNote: PDF extraction shows bare ت without a clear kasra; form reconstructed from conjugation system as ـتِ (الْمُخَاطَبَة).

items.note: This is تَاء التَّأْنِيث (sign of femininity), not a pronoun, but it identifies الْغَائِبَة.

items.unclearNote: PDF extraction shows bare ت without a clear fatha; form reconstructed as ـتَ (الْمُخَاطَب).
PDF extraction shows ـتُمْ for this item, which duplicates the worked example. Based on the conjugation system, the missing sigha is الْمُتَكَلِّمُوْن (ـنَا). Verify against the printed book.

items.note: Same ending ـتُمَا as item 7; this one refers to الْمُخَاطَبَتَان.

exercises.instructionText: Translate the following, write the صِيْغَة, and point out the ضَمِيْر.

items.unclearNote: PDF extraction shows ذَهَبَت without a visible sukoon on the final ت. Form reconstructed as ذَهَبَتْ based on context.
PDF extraction shows رَكَعْت without a clear fatha on the final ت. Reconstructed as رَكَعْتَ (الْمُخَاطَب).
PDF extraction shows جَعَلْت as fragmented text (ت ل جَع). Reconstructed as جَعَلْتِ (الْمُخَاطَبَة) based on context.
PDF extraction shows فَتَحْتُن without the shadda on ن. Reconstructed as فَتَحْتُنَّ.

exercises.instructionText: Translate the following and write their صِيْغَة.
exercises.note: All verbs are from بَاب فَتَح. Vocabulary from page 22.

exercises.instructionText: Change the gender of the following صِيَغ of الْمَاضِي whilst keeping the number and person the same.
exercises.note: الْمُتَكَلِّمُوْن and الْمُتَكَلِّم are shared between masculine and feminine; their forms do not change. الْمُخَاطَبَان and الْمُخَاطَبَتَان share the same ـتُمَا ending.

answer.note: Form is identical — both dual second-person sighas share ـتُمَا.
الْمُتَكَلِّمُوْن is shared for both genders; form does not change.
الْمُتَكَلِّمُوْن is shared for both genders; form does not change.

items.note: Uses the generic فَعَلَ pattern verb, not a vocabulary word.

exercises.instructionText: Practice verbally conjugating the following verbs of الْمُضَارِع. Thereafter, fill in the table below.
exercises.note: الغَائِب row is given; students fill the remaining 13 sigha rows. All four verbs are from بَاب فَتَح (يَفْعَلُ pattern).

exercises.instructionText: Write the صِيْغَة and English pronoun corresponding to the following حَرْف الْمُضَارِع and pronoun.
exercises.note: Items show حَرْف الْمُضَارِع combined with the visible pronoun suffix. Some patterns are inherently ambiguous (shared by multiple sighas). الْمُخَاطَبُوْن (تَـ + ـُوْن) is the worked example.

items.note: Inherently ambiguous: both الْغَائِبَة and الْمُخَاطَب share the pattern تَفْعَلُ.
Inherently ambiguous: three sighas share the pattern تَفْعَلَان.
مَبْنِي — form does not change with irab.

items.unclearNote: PDF extraction shows this item as تَـ + ـُوْن (identical to the worked example). Based on the logic of the exercise — all 14 sighas must be covered — the missing sigha is الْمُتَكَلِّم (أَفْعَلُ, pattern أَـ + ـُ). Verify against the printed book.

items.note: مَبْنِي — form does not change with irab.

exercises.instructionText: Translate the following, write the صِيْغَة, and point out the ضَمِيْر.
exercises.note: All verbs are from بَاب فَتَح. Some forms are inherently ambiguous between two sighas.

answer.english: You (m/p) bow / are bowing / will bow
You (f/d) send OR They (f/d) send / are sending / will send

items.note: Form تَفْعَلَان is shared by الْغَائِبَتَان, الْمُخَاطَبَان, and الْمُخَاطَبَتَان.

answer.english: We elevate / are elevating / will elevate
They (m/d) curse / are cursing / will curse
They (m/p) read / are reading / will read

items.unclearNote: PDF extraction shows وْن ؤ ُ يَقْر (garbled). Reconstructed as يَقْرَءُوْن based on context.

answer.english: She goes / is going / will go  OR  You (m/s) go / are going / will go

items.note: Inherently ambiguous: تَفْعَلُ serves both الْغَائِبَة and الْمُخَاطَب.

answer.english: They (f/d) elevate OR You (m/d) elevate OR You (f/d) elevate
answer.sigha: الْغَائِبَتَان أَو الْمُخَاطَبَان أَو الْمُخَاطَبَتَان مِن الْمُضَارِع

items.note: Inherently ambiguous: three sighas share تَفْعَلَان.

answer.english: You (f/p) make / are making / will make
She acts righteously  OR  You (m/s) act righteously

items.note: Inherently ambiguous.

answer.english: I ask / am asking / will ask
He bows / is bowing / will bow
He benefits / is benefiting / will benefit
They (f/d) bewitch OR You (m/d) bewitch OR You (f/d) bewitch
answer.sigha: الْغَائِبَتَان أَو الْمُخَاطَبَان أَو الْمُخَاطَبَتَان مِن الْمُضَارِع

items.note: Inherently ambiguous.

answer.english: They (f/p) ask / are asking / will ask

exercises.instructionText: Complete the table below.
exercises.note: Students write both the مضارع معلوم and مضارع مجهول forms for each English prompt.

exercises.instructionText: State whether the following patterns are مَاضٍ or مُضَارِعٌ; and مَعْلُوْمٌ or مَجْهُوْلٌ.

example.description: ضمة on first root letter, كسرة on second, وا ending

identificationKey.madiMaloom: عَيْن has فتحة/كسرة/ضمة in standard pattern (no internal ضمة+كسرة sequence)
identificationKey.madiMajhool: first root letter has ضمة + second has كسرة (فُعِلَ template)
identificationKey.mudariMajhool: حرف المضارع has ضمة (يُـ/تُـ/أُـ/نُـ) + penultimate letter has فتحة

exercises.note: PDF extraction of these abstract patterns is unreliable; all items flagged unclear. Answers are best-effort based on the systematic coverage of patterns expected in a 20-item exercise.

items.unclearNote: PDF shows ـت ـُ ـِ ـ without unambiguous harakah on final ت. Damma reconstructed = متكلم (فُعِلْتُ). Could be مخاطب (فُعِلْتَ) — verify item 4 vs 9 against book.
PDF shows ـتْ ـُ ـِ ـ — sukoon on ت reconstructed as تاء التأنيث = الغائبة.
PDF shows ـ ـِ ـْن ـت — kasra on عين letter extracted clearly; pattern from باب ضَرَب/حَسِب. Still مضارع معلوم regardless of بَاب. Verify kasra vs fatha against book.
PDF shows same fragment as item 4 (ـت ـُ ـِ ـ). Fatha reconstructed = مخاطب. Verify harakah on ت against book.

items.sigha_note: الْمُخَاطَبُوْن (باب كَرُم pattern — fatha+damma on root letters ≠ ضمة+كسرة majhool signature)
items.unclearNote: PDF shows ـتُم ـَ ـُ ـ — fatha+damma on root letters reconstructed as فَعُلَ (bab karam) = maloom. Would be مجهول if ضمة+كسرة; verify harakah on root letters against book.
PDF shows ـتُن ـِ ـُ ـ — shadda on نَّ missing in extraction. Reconstructed as فُعِلْتُنَّ.
PDF shows تُمَا ـُ ـِ ـ — pronoun تُمَا and ضمة+كسرة on root letters clearly indicate مجهول.

exercises.unclearNote: Items 4, 7, 8, 9, 12, 19, 20 have garbled harakah in PDF extraction. Tense/voice answers are confident; exact sigha identifications are flagged.

recognitionRules.madiMaloom: no حرف المضارع prefix; root vowels follow فَعَلَ/فَعِلَ/فَعُلَ pattern (no ضمة+كسرة sequence)
recognitionRules.madiMajhool: no حرف المضارع prefix; first root letter has ضمة, second has كسرة (فُعِلَ template)

distribution.note: Distribution is intentionally uneven — exercise focuses on الْمَجْهُوْل patterns (new content) alongside familiar مضارع معلوم patterns.

exercises.instructionText: State the صِيْغَة and the grammatical state of the following verbs.
exercises.note: All items are مضارع. Each must be identified by: (1) صِيْغَة, (2) grammatical state (مَرْفُوْع / مَنْصُوْب / مَجْزُوْم). الغَائِبَات and الْمُخَاطَبَات are مَبْنِي and do not change. Items with لَمْ → مَجْزُوْم; with لَنْ → مَنْصُوْب; with لَ (نَفْي) → مَرْفُوْع; bare → مَرْفُوْع.

items.unclearNote: PDF shows عْقِلْن ي لَن (garbled RTL). Reconstructed as لَنْ يَعْقِلْنَ (الْغَائِبَات — مَبْنِي).

items.note: تَفْعَلَا is shared by three sighas; all three are valid answers.
Ambiguous: تَفْعَلَ is shared by الْغَائِبَة and الْمُخَاطَب.
Ambiguous form.

items.unclearNote: PDF shows تَجْعَل َ لَم — the fatha on ل may indicate مَنْصُوْب or may be a PDF artefact. Reconstructed as لَمْ تَجْعَلْ (مَجْزُوْم, sukoon). Verify against book.

items.note: Bare مُضَارِع with نُوْن الإِعْرَاب → مَرْفُوْع.

items.unclearNote: PDF shows يَّكْرُمُوْا لَن — the shadda on ي is a PDF artefact. Reconstructed as لَنْ يَكْرُمُوا.
PDF shows مَا حْر ي لَم (garbled). Reconstructed as لَمْ يَحْرُمْ (الْغَائِب).

items.note: مَجْهُوْل form — تُفْعَلُوْن pattern.

exercises.instructionText: Make the following verbs negative and then translate.
exercises.note: All items are ماضي. Negative particle for ماضي is مَا. Students prefix مَا and translate the resulting negative sentence.

items.unclearNote: PDF shows جَعَلْت without clear fatha on ت. Reconstructed as جَعَلْتَ (الْمُخَاطَب).

answer.english: You (m/d or f/d) did not elevate

items.unclearNote: PDF shows عْتُمَا ف ر (garbled RTL). Reconstructed as رَفَعْتُمَا.
PDF shows لْن أ س (garbled). Reconstructed as سَأَلْنَ.
PDF shows وْا ؤ قَر (garbled). Reconstructed as قَرَءُوا.

PDF shows نَا ع ل (garbled). This appears to be لَعَنَّا → more likely لَعَنَّا or رَفَعْنَا. Given the vocabulary set and exercise context, reconstructed as لَعَنَّا but verify against book — the Arabic root is likely لَعَنَ (to curse).

exercises.instructionText: Translate the following. If there is more than one possible translation, mention all of them.
exercises.note: All items are مضارع منفي. Particles: لَمْ (past negative, مجزوم), مَا (present negative, مرفوع), لَ (present/future negative, مرفوع), لَنْ (future emphatic negative, منصوب). Some forms are مجهول.

answer.english: They (m/d) do not ride / are not riding / will not ride
answer.note: لَا here is حَرْف نَفْي → مرفوع. The نُوْن الإِعْرَاب remains.

answer.english: They (m/p) do not become grieved / are not becoming grieved

answer.sigha: الْغَائِبَة أَو الْمُخَاطَب مِن الْمُضَارِع الْمَنْصُوْب
answer.note: Ambiguous: تَفْعَلَ shared by الْغَائِبَة and الْمُخَاطَب.

answer.english: They (f/d) / You (m/d) / You (f/d) do not bear witness / are not bearing witness / will not bear witness
answer.sigha: الْغَائِبَتَان أَو الْمُخَاطَبَان أَو الْمُخَاطَبَتَان مِن الْمُضَارِع الْمَرْفُوْع

answer.english: They (f/p) do not stay / remain  OR  are not staying / remaining
You (f/p) do not do / are not doing / will not do
We are not shown mercy / We are not being shown mercy
He / It will not be carried (emphatic — with نُوْن التَّوْكِيد)
answer.note: This item contains نُوْن التَّوْكِيد الثَّقِيلَة which is not covered until Unit 12. Flag for teacher attention.

items.unclearNote: PDF shows ل َ يُحْمَلَن — not clear whether final letter is نُوْن التَّوْكِيد or نُوْن النِّسْوَة. Reconstructed as لَا يُحْمَلَنَّ based on context.

exercises.instructionText: Make the following verbs negative by prefixing the حَرْفُ النَّفْي given in brackets. Adjust the irab of the verb where necessary.
exercises.note: Particle given in brackets. Where particle is لَمْ → verb becomes مجزوم (drop نُوْن الإِعْرَاب or add سُكُون). Where لَنْ → verb becomes منصوب (drop نُوْن الإِعْرَاب or add فتحة). Where مَا or لَا (نَفْي) → verb stays مرفوع. Some items are ماضي (مَا only).

answer.english: They (m/d) cannot / do not have power
He did not bear with patience
She / You (m/s) does not earn / is not earning
They (f/d) / You (m/d) / You (f/d) will not own (emphatic)

items.unclearNote: PDF shows هَبَطتُّم (extra shadda). Reconstructed as هَبَطْتُمْ.

answer.irabChange: مَرْفُوْع — no change (but نُوْن retained only in مرفوع; this form is already مجزوم-shaped — see note)
answer.english: You (f/s) are not helped / will not be helped

items.note: لَا here is حَرْف نَفْي → مرفوع. تُنْصَرِيْن is already the مرفوع form of الْمُخَاطَبَة from الْمُضَارِع الْمَجْهُوْل.

answer.english: You (m/d or f/d) did not create

items.unclearNote: PDF shows لَقْتُمَا خ (garbled). Reconstructed as خَلَقْتُمَا.

answer.english: You (f/p) do not worship / will not worship

exercises.instructionText: Practice verbally conjugating the following verbs of الْأَمْر. Thereafter, fill in the table below.
exercises.note: الْمُخَاطَب row is given with all five أَمْر verbs. Students fill الْمُخَاطَبَان, الْمُخَاطَبُوْن, الْمُخَاطَبَة, الْمُخَاطَبَتَان, الْمُخَاطَبَات rows. الْأَمْر only exists for مُخَاطَب sighas.

exercises.instructionText: Translate the verbs below into English and write the صِيْغَة.
exercises.note: All items are أَمْر. Many are Quranic (marked with ﴿﴾). Translation = imperative English. Some items have prefixes (وَ، فَ) which are conjunctions — strip for translation but note them.

exercises.instructionText: Translate the verbs below into Arabic and write the صِيْغَة.
exercises.note: All items are أَمْر. Vocabulary from بَاب ضَرَب, بَاب نَصَر, بَاب سَمِع, بَاب فَتَح. هَمْزَة الْوَصْل carries كَسْرَة unless عَيْن has ضَمَّة.

answer.note: هَمْزَة الْوَصْل has ضَمَّة because عَيْن المضارع has ضَمَّة.

exercises.instructionText: Practice verbally conjugating the following verbs of النَّهْي. Thereafter, fill in the table below.
exercises.note: الْمُخَاطَب row is given. Students fill the remaining 5 مُخَاطَب sigha rows. النَّهْي = لَا + مضارع مجزوم.

exercises.instructionText: Translate the verbs below into English and write the صِيْغَة.
exercises.note: All items are نَهْي (لَا + مضارع مجزوم). Many are Quranic. Translation = 'Do not …'.

answer.english: Do not come out! (m/d or f/d)

items.unclearNote: PDF shows جَا ل َ تَخْر (garbled). Reconstructed as لَا تَخْرُجَا.

exercises.instructionText: Translate the verbs below into Arabic and write the صِيْغَة.
exercises.note: All items are نَهْي. Form = لَا + مضارع المخاطب مجزوم.

exercises.instructionText: Translate the following. Ensure you differentiate between the different types of verbs.
exercises.note: Mixed exercise: ماضي معلوم, ماضي مجهول, مضارع معلوم, مضارع مجهول, أَمْر, نَهْي, مضارع منفي (لَا/لَمْ/لَنْ/مَا). All items use the root سَمِعَ/سَمِع or رَحِمَ/رَحِم/لَعَنَ — grouped in three triplets. Students must identify verb type precisely.

answer.english: You (m/p) hear / are hearing / will hear
You (m/p) are heard / are being heard / will be heard
You (m/p) do not hear / are not hearing / will not hear

items.note: لَا here is حَرْف نَفْي (not نَهْي) — verb is مَرْفُوْع, نُوْن الإِعْرَاب retained.

answer.english: She has mercy / is having mercy / will have mercy  OR  You (m/s) have mercy
Do not have mercy! (m/s)  OR  Do not have mercy! (f/s inferred)
She / You (m/s) was not shown mercy
She / You (m/s) will certainly not be shown mercy
She / You (m/s) is shown mercy / is being shown mercy / will be shown mercy
He curses / is cursing / will curse
He is cursed / is being cursed / will be cursed
He is not cursed / will not be cursed

items.unclearNote: PDF shows لْعَن ي ل (garbled). Reconstructed as لَا يُلْعَنُ based on the pattern of the exercise (negated مجهول مضارع for الغائب).

exercises.instructionText: Practice verbally conjugating the following اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل. Thereafter, fill in the table below.
exercises.note: الْمُفْرَد row is given. Students fill الْمُثَنَّى (m/d), جَمْع الْمُذَكَّر (m/p), الْمُفْرَدَة (f/s), الْمُثَنَّاة (f/d), جَمْع الْمُؤَنَّث (f/p). Pattern: فَاعِل → فَاعِلَان → فَاعِلُوْن → فَاعِلَة → فَاعِلَتَان → فَاعِلَات. Note from book: words from بَاب كَرُم do not follow this pattern (e.g. كَرِيْم).

exercises.instructionText: Translate the following into English and write the صِيْغَة.
exercises.note: All items are اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل (active participle). Translation uses: verbal meaning (is doing / going to do), noun meaning (the one who does / those who do), or adjective. Items with ال are typically used as nouns.

items.unclearNote: PDF shows قَاتِلَن — final letter unclear. Reconstructed as قَاتِلَان (m/d, الْمُثَنَّى) as it is the only form not represented by other items. Verify against book.

answer.english: Those who bear patience (f/p) / patient women
Those who oppress (f/d) / two oppressing women

items.unclearNote: PDF shows ات ذَاكِر (garbled RTL). Reconstructed as ذَاكِرَات.

answer.english: The one who forgives (m/s) / the forgiving one

items.unclearNote: PDF shows تَان القَادِر (garbled RTL). Reconstructed as الْقَادِرَتَان.

answer.sigha: جَمْع الْمُذَكَّر (with ال — used as noun)

answer.english: One who owns (f/s) / one who has ability (f/s)

answer.sigha: الْمُفْرَد (with ال — used as Divine Name/noun)

answer.english: The judge / the one who judges (m/s)
Those who help (f/d) / two helping women

exercises.instructionText: Translate the following into Arabic and write the صِيْغَة.
exercises.note: All answers are اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل (فَاعِل pattern). Vocabulary from Unit 1.

items.note: Instruction text reads 'make' — likely a typo for 'makes' in the book. Answer is the same.

exercises.instructionText: Practice verbally conjugating the following اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل. Thereafter, fill in the table below.
exercises.note: الْمُفْرَد row is given. Students fill الْمُثَنَّى (m/d), جَمْع الْمُذَكَّر (m/p), الْمُفْرَدَة (f/s), الْمُثَنَّاة (f/d), جَمْع الْمُؤَنَّث (f/p). Pattern: مَفْعُوْل → مَفْعُوْلَان → مَفْعُوْلُوْن → مَفْعُوْلَة → مَفْعُوْلَتَان → مَفْعُوْلَات. Uses same 4 root verbs as Ex 1 (بَلَغَ، عَبَدَ، ذَكَرَ، رَزَقَ).
exercises.pdfExtractionWarning: PDF extraction shows 'اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل' as column headers and 'بَالِغ، عَابِد، ذَاكِر، رَازِق' (اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل forms) in the given row — this is a known RTL table extraction error. The actual book table has اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل headers and مَبْلُوغ، مَعْبُود، مَذْكُور، مَرْزُوق as the given الْمُفْرَد row. All items flagged unclear — verify against book.
exercises.unclearNote: Entire exercise flagged unclear due to PDF table extraction error on column headers and given row. Answers are computed correctly from the مَفْعُوْل pattern applied to بَلَغَ، عَبَدَ، ذَكَرَ، رَزَقَ. Verify given row (الْمُفْرَد) against book.
exercises.pdfDiscrepancyNote: PDF table headers and first-row data both show اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل labels (بَالِغ، عَابِد، ذَاكِر، رَازِق) — identical to Exercise 1 (p66). This is a PDF extraction error caused by RTL table column misread. The exercise INSTRUCTION unambiguously says اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل, so the correct given words are the مَفْعُوْل forms: مَبْلُوغ، مَعْبُود، مَذْكُور، مَرْزُوق. Verify against the physical book.
exercises.pattern_note: Pattern: مَفْعُوْل (مُفْرَد) → مَفْعُوْلَة (مُفْرَدَة) → مَفْعُوْلَان (مُثَنَّى) → مَفْعُوْلَتَان (مُثَنَّاة) → مَفْعُوْلُوْن (جَمْع مُذَكَّر) → مَفْعُوْلَات (جَمْع مُؤَنَّث)

exercises.instructionText: Translate the verbs below into English and write the صِيْغَة.
exercises.note: All items are اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل (passive participle). Translation: 'one who is …-ed', 'those who are …-ed', or used verbally as 'is being …-ed / going to be …-ed'.

items.unclearNote: PDF shows تَان مَحْشُوْر (garbled RTL). Reconstructed as مَحْشُورَتَان.
PDF shows ظْلُوْمُوْن م (garbled). Reconstructed as مَظْلُوْمُوْن.
PDF shows مَعْقُوْلَت (missing final alif). Reconstructed as مَعْقُوْلَات.
PDF shows بْعُوْثُوْن م (garbled). Reconstructed as مَبْعُوْثُوْن.

exercises.instructionText: Translate the verbs below into Arabic and write the صِيْغَة.
exercises.note: All answers are اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل (مَفْعُوْل pattern). 'Going to be asked' uses the verbal meaning of اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل (near future with passive sense).

answer.note: Uses verbal meaning of اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل — near future passive.

exercises.instructionText: Fill in the table below by completing the تَصْرِيف صَغِيْر of the following مَصْدَر.
exercises.note: The مَصْدَر is given (column 3 / column 6). Students fill the remaining 8 columns. Table columns right-to-left: (1) الْمَاضِي الْمَعْلُوْم | (2) الْمُضَارِع الْمَعْلُوْم | (3) الْمَصْدَر | (4) الْمَاضِي الْمَجْهُوْل | (5) الْمُضَارِع الْمَجْهُوْل | (6) الْمَصْدَر | (7) الْأَمْر | (8) النَّهْي | (9) اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل | (10) اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل. بَاب كَرُم has no مجهول and no اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل; اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل follows a different pattern (e.g. كَبِير). The given مَصَادِر identify both the verb and its بَاب.

referenceTable.note: Completed example rows from page 70 (for reference)

answer.note: بَاب كَرُم has no مجهول form and no standard اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل. اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل follows the كَرِيْم pattern (فَعِيْل), not فَاعِل.

exercises.instructionText: Practice the تَصْرِيف كَبِيْر with two verbs from each بَاب.
exercises.note: No written table to fill. Students choose any two vocabulary verbs from each of the 6 أَبْوَاب and orally produce the full 14-sigha conjugation across all columns. The complete تَصْرِيف كَبِيْر table is shown on page 71 using فَتَحَ as the example, with 8 columns: الْمَاضِي الْمَعْلُوْم | الْمُضَارِع الْمَعْلُوْم | الْمَاضِي الْمَجْهُوْل | الْمُضَارِع الْمَجْهُوْل | الْأَمْر | النَّهْي | اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل | اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل.

referenceTable.note: Full تَصْرِيف كَبِيْر for فَتَحَ as shown on page 71 (14 sigha rows × 8 columns). Only الْمُخَاطَب-through-الْمُتَكَلِّمُوْن rows include أَمْر، نَهْي، اِسْمُ الْفَاعِل، اِسْمُ الْمَفْعُوْل per the layout shown.
